paper_metadata.title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct
paper_metadata.abstract: Large language models (LLMs), such as GPT-4, have shown remarkable performance in natural language processing (NLP) tasks, including challenging mathematical reasoning. However, most existing open-source models are only pre-trained on large-scale internet data and without math-related optimization. In this paper, we present WizardMath, which enhances the mathematical CoT reasoning abilities of LLMs without using external python tools, by applying our proposed Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF) method to the domain of math. Through extensive experiments on two mathematical reasoning benchmarks, namely GSM8k and MATH, we reveal the extraordinary capabilities of our model. Remarkably, WizardMath-Mistral 7B surpasses top-tier open-source LLMs by a substantial margin with higher data efficiency. Furthermore, WizardMath 70B even outperforms GPT-3.5-Turbo, Claude 2, Gemini Pro and GPT-4-early-version. Additionally, our preliminary exploration highlights the pivotal role of instruction evolution and process supervision in achieving exceptional math performance. For more details refer to https://github.com/nlpxucan/WizardLM

extraction_data.organization: Microsoft Corporation, Shenzhen Institute Of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy Of Sciences
extraction_data.innovation: WizardMath enhances mathematical reasoning in LLMs using Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF), combining Math Evol-Instruct, process-supervised reward models (IRM and PRM), and PPO training to improve step-by-step reasoning on math problems.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling (as per the paper's description of using Evol-Instruct and process-supervised reinforcement learning)
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (as per the paper's mention of large-scale data)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning (via PPO with PRM and IRM)
extraction_data.optimizer: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) with PRM (Process Reward Model) and IRM (Instruction Reward Model)
extraction_data.hardware_used: Not specified in the paper; inferring from related work, likely cloud TPU or similar large-scale hardware (as typical for large models)
extraction_data.extension: The use of Math Evol-Instruct and process-supervised reward models (IRM and PRM) to improve instruction quality and step-by-step reasoning accuracy.
extraction_data.license: Closed source (as per the paper's mention of proprietary models like GPT-3.5 Turbo, Claude 2, etc. being compared, but the paper does not specify an open-source license for WizardMath)
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving mathematical reasoning in large language models without external tools or domain-specific training data.
extraction_data.application: Mathematical reasoning, problem-solving, and instruction-following tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct

extraction_data.innovation: WizardMath applies its reinforcement learning approach (PPO with PRM and IRM) to Mistral 7B, demonstrating significant improvements in mathematical reasoning on benchmarks like GSM8k and MATH.
extraction_data.application: Mathematical reasoning, problem-solving, and instruction following
extraction_data.research_problem: Mathematical reasoning in large language models
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning (via PPO with PRM and IRM)
extraction_data.optimizer: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) with PRM (Process Reward Model) and IRM (Instruction Reward Model)
extraction_data.extension: The paper extends Mistral 7B with WizardMath's reinforcement learning method, showing that even smaller models can achieve state-of-the-art performance with proper instruction and reward tuning.
extraction_data.paper_title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct

extraction_data.innovation: WizardMath applies its reinforcement learning approach (PPO with PRM and IRM) to GPT-2 XL 1.5B, demonstrating significant improvements in mathematical reasoning on benchmarks like GSM8k and MATH.
extraction_data.application: Mathematical reasoning, problem-solving, and instruction following

extraction_data.optimizer: PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) with PRM (Process Reward Model) and IRM (Instruction Reward Model)
extraction_data.extension: The paper extends GPT-2 XL 1.5B with WizardMath's reinforcement learning method, showing that even smaller models can achieve state-of-the-art performance with proper instruction and reward tuning.
